sources.name: How to Solve the Alignment Problem (Carlsmith)
sources.notes: 9 essays. Primary source for Rogue AI category refinement: rogue vs misuse distinction, hand-off framing, option control vs motivation control, global vulnerability conditions.

sources.name: Otherness and Control in the Age of AGI (Carlsmith)
sources.notes: 192 pages. Philosophy of human-AI relations. Deep atheism, yin/yang, gentleness toward AI as 'other', liberalism/boundaries, attunement. Challenges pure control framing.

sources.name: How to be a wise optimist about science and technology (Nielsen)
sources.notes: Wise optimism: accepts xrisk, works to overcome it. Armageddon vs Kumbaya heuristics, market vs non-market safety, coceleration, hyper-entities.

sources.name: Better Futures (MacAskill)
sources.notes: 6 essays. Primary source for Flourishing category: S×F framework, multiplicative model, path dependence, convergence skepticism.

sources.name: The 2025 Peregrine Report
sources.notes: 208 proposals extracted, converted to problem/intervention entries via tiling tree method

sources.name: CLTR Defensive Acceleration List (UK)
sources.notes: 29 UK-centric interventions. Mostly UK-specific policy/systems. ~5 map to existing entries, ~5 could become new generalizable interventions.

sources.name: Convergent Research Gap Map
sources.notes: 101 R&D gaps across 17 fields. WARNING: Most not relevant (astrophysics, space, ecology). Filter for: AI safety, biosecurity, global health.

sources.name: MIT AI Risk Repository
sources.notes: Used for taxonomy stress-test. 7 domains, 23 sub-categories. Confirms coverage, no critical gaps. Hyperwar potentially underspecified.

sources.name: Epistemic Security (early thinking)
sources.notes: Already a tiling tree! Source integrity → content integrity → distribution governance → coordination infrastructure. Could become a problem entry directly.

sources.name: Project GRASP (CeSIA)
sources.notes: French AI Safety Center taxonomy. All 41 solutions already covered by existing entries. Template extractions deleted.

sources.name: Achieving a Secure AI Agent Ecosystem
sources.notes: Schmidt Sciences report on AI agent security.

sources.name: Sam Bowman's AI Safety Checklist
sources.notes: 25 goals across 3 chapters. Most map to existing 4 problems. Anthropic-specific framing (ASL levels, RSP). No new problems identified.

sources.name: List of Lists of Biosecurity Projects
sources.notes: Meta-list processed. 4 high-priority sub-lists added as separate sources. Most consensus projects already covered.

sources.name: Concrete Biosecurity Projects (EA Forum)
sources.notes: 6 biosecurity projects. Most map to terrorism/extinction (detection, countermeasures, refuges). BWC Strengthening maps to proliferation. Gap: no proposals address AI-bio intersection.

sources.name: Apollo Program for Biodefense
sources.notes: 4 goals: detection, testing, treatment, vaccines—all by 2030. All map to terrorism/extinction. $10B/year. Traditional biosecurity (no AI-bio focus). PDF has more tech detail.

sources.name: Technologies to Address GCBRs (Johns Hopkins)
sources.notes: Site returns 403. Manual PDF download required. Expected: tech inventory for terrorism/extinction interventions.

sources.name: Biosecurity Needs Engineers (EA Forum)
sources.notes: 7 hardware interventions: PPE, Far-UVC, ventilation, UVGI, lab safety, biomonitoring, vehicle filtration. All terrorism/extinction except lab safety (proliferation). Engineering skill-gap framing.

sources.name: Atlas AI Resilience Gap Map
sources.notes: 119 gaps in main sheet. Check 'Related lists' tab for more sources.

sources.name: Atlas Cybersecurity Projects
sources.notes: 23 fundable cybersecurity projects. Convergent Research willing to house efforts.

sources.name: List of Lists of AI Safety Projects
sources.notes: Meta-list processed. 3 high-priority sub-lists added. Heavy overlap with existing taxonomy.

sources.name: 100+ Concrete Projects in Evals
sources.notes: 100+ projects across scheming, autonomy, AI R&D, control, bio/cyber/persuasion, multi-agent. Maps to detect-evaluation-sandbagging, safety-benchmark-development, end-to-end-harm-assessment. Strong overlap with existing entries.

sources.name: Redwood Research Control Project Proposals
sources.notes: 36 proposals, 10 tractable directions. Strong mapping to contain-unsafe-ai, multi-agent-threat-monitoring, detect-evaluation-sandbagging. 3 potential sub-interventions: synthetic honeypots, collusion prevention, elicitation without learning.

sources.name: Ten AI Safety Projects (Julian Hazell)
sources.notes: Funder perspective on fundable org ideas. 6 map to existing interventions, 4 new candidates (comms, lit reviews, resilience plan, econ tracker).

sources.name: d/acc: Defensive Acceleration (Vitalik Buterin)
sources.notes: 3 essays. d/acc (defensive/decentralization/differential acceleration). Offense/defense balance, four domains (macro, bio, cyber, info). Defense-favoring worlds enable better governance. Balvi bio-defense work.

sources.name: Dynamism for AI Safety (Helen Toner)
sources.notes: 4 essays. Dynamism vs stasis (Virginia Postrel), stasist tendencies in AI safety, alignment as steerability, concentration-of-power risks, dynamist rules. Former OpenAI board member.

sources.name: AI Futures Project
sources.notes: 12 files. Daniel Kokotajlo (ex-OpenAI), Eli Lifland, Scott Alexander. AI 2027 scenarios, short timelines (modal 2027), fast takeoff, CEO takeover pathways, multi-AI conflict, Plans A/B/C/D framework, policy priorities. Key insight: threats are entangled, even 'good' scenarios involve massive power concentration.
